title: ChamferFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a chamfer feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setToDistanceAndAngle
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a set of edges to this input.

methods.name: setToEqualDistance
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a set of edges to this input.

methods.name: setToTwoDistances
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a set of edges to this input.

properties.name: chamferEdgeSets
properties.description: Returns the collection of edge sets for this chamfer feature input.

properties.name: cornerType
properties.description: Gets and sets the type of corner to be modeled when multiple edges connect at a vertex.

properties.name: edges
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets the collection of edges that will be chamfered.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: isTangentChain
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets if any edges that are tangentially connected to any of chamfered edges will also be included in the chamfer.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Equal Distance Chamfer Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates an equal distance chamfer on the selected edge. If there are tangent contiguous edges that will also be included in the chamfer.

chamferEdgeSets.title: ChamferFeatureInput.chamferEdgeSets Property
chamferEdgeSets.description: Returns the collection of edge sets for this chamfer feature input.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = chamferFeatureInput_var.chamferEdgeSets

related_samples.name: chamferFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the chamferFeatures.add method. To use this sample have a part open that contains a body. When you run the script, you will be prompted to select an edge to chamfer.

related_samples.name: Equal Distance Chamfer Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates an equal distance chamfer on the selected edge. If there are tangent contiguous edges that will also be included in the chamfer.

cornerType.title: ChamferFeatureInput.cornerType Property
cornerType.description: Gets and sets the type of corner to be modeled when multiple edges connect at a vertex.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = chamferFeatureInput_var.cornerType

# Set the value of the property.
chamferFeatureInput_var.cornerType = propertyValue

To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

classType.title: ChamferFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setToDistanceAndAngle.title: ChamferFeatureInput.setToDistanceAndAngle Method
setToDistanceAndAngle.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a set of edges to this input.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the distance of the chamfer. This distance is along the face which is on the right of the selected edge. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: A valueInput object that defines the angle. The direction will be towards to the face which is on the left of the selected edge. This can be a string or a value. If it's a string it is interpreted using the current document units and can include equations. For example all of the following are valid as long as they result in angle units; "45", "45 deg", "a1 / 2". If a value is input it is interpreted as radians. It cannot be negative.

setToEqualDistance.title: ChamferFeatureInput.setToEqualDistance Method
setToEqualDistance.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a set of edges to this input.
setToEqualDistance.return_description: Returns true if the set of edges was successfully added to the ChamferFeatureInput.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the size of the chamfer. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

setToTwoDistances.title: ChamferFeatureInput.setToTwoDistances Method
setToTwoDistances.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a set of edges to this input.

parameters.name: distanceOne
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the distanceOne of the chamfer. This distance is along the face which is on the left of the selected edge. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: distanceTwo
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the distanceTwo of the chamfer. This distance is along the face which is on the right of the selected edge. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in"). If no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.
